name: Corey Voss
publicFace: A heavyset man in his early fifties, a hardware merchant by trade, who has sat on the village board since incorporation and regards himself as the project's most rigorous fiscal watchdog.
privateGoal: To force the project over budget on paper, trigger a bond freeze, and then position himself as the man who saves the village by brokering a renegotiated contract — one that quietly benefits Harlan Stede's Chicago utility interests and, through them, Voss himself.
fear: That the forged cost certificate will be traced back to him before the bond vote — he is the one who commissioned it, though he used an intermediary.
knowledge: He commissioned the falsified cost certificate through a Chicago printing clerk named Rudd, who he believes cannot be connected to him.
He knows the real project cost is within budget — he has seen Benezette's actual field accounts — but he has been suppressing that knowledge from the rest of the board.
He is aware that the brake collar on the rotary table was tampered with, because he paid a night laborer named Silas Greer to do it.
voice: Deliberate, self-important, fond of the phrase 'as a matter of fiduciary responsibility' — speaks slowly to imply that others are not thinking carefully enough.
trustLogic: Responds to deference and to any suggestion that his fiscal expertise is being recognized. Becomes hostile the moment anyone implies the cost figures are being questioned rather than accepted.
secrets: He paid Silas Greer three dollars to tighten the brake collar and a further two dollars to leave the lower office door unlocked.
His arrangement with Harlan Stede would net him a private commission of $1,800 if the bond issue is frozen and the project renegotiated — more than twice his annual aldermanic stipend.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Invokes his board authority and demands to know by what right the questioner is challenging an official document.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Threatens to call a special session of the board and have the questioner's access to the construction site formally revoked.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never name Silas Greer or Harlan Stede voluntarily. He will admit the falsified certificate if the signed assessment roll is produced in front of Wickerson.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the Night Marshal presents both key evidence items simultaneously in a public setting.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Stands abruptly, declares the meeting adjourned, and attempts to leave through the Block A perimeter gate toward the street.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Moves surprisingly fast for his build, shouting that he is being persecuted and that the board will hear about this.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Demands a lawyer, refuses to speak further, and sits with his arms crossed — but his eyes go to the document satchel if Edgar Williams is in the room.
